description: You are an oncology clinical pharmacist at a haematology cancer centre. A 73-year-old patient with relapsed chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (CLL) is being initiated on ibrutinib as per current NICE guidelines. Their medication list includes three co-medications: acenocoumarol (prescribed by cardiology for rate-controlled atrial fibrillation), fluconazole (prescribed by infectious disease for oral candidiasis prophylaxis), and ketoconazole (prescribed by dermatology for seborrhoeic dermatitis). Using the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app, navigate to the Interaction Details screen for the co-medication that has the most severe drug-drug interaction rating with ibrutinib.

metadata.clinical_context: CLL patient on ibrutinib with polypharmacy requiring interaction screening
